**Curriculum Vitae of Yuan Lee**  
![][image1]

1. **PERSONAL DETAILS**

Full name: Yuan Lee   
Nationality: South Korea   
Marital status: Unmarried    
Year of birth: 1982  
Mobile m: \+82 010 5759 5485   
Email: [totitre@naver.com](mailto:totitre@naver.com) ; [healthylee7@gmail.com](mailto:healthylee7@gmail.com)   
School/Department Homepage and Faculty Profile in Korean   
[https://future.silla.ac.kr/future/index.php?pCode=SUB1000003\&pg=2\&mode=view\&idx=176](https://future.silla.ac.kr/future/index.php?pCode=SUB1000003&pg=2&mode=view&idx=176)  
Religion: Catholic    
Hobby: Cycling  

**Current positions**  
Currently, I work as a sales and marketing associate at Jeongsang Bukhan Mountain Resort, while also teaching tourism-related courses as an adjunct professor at Silla University.

**A-1. Education**

| Period | Content  |
| :---- | :---- |
| 1997\~1999 | Completed Teresa High school, Pusan |
| 2000.3.2\~2007.2.26 | Awarded Bachelor Degree in Educational Technology (Major) English literature (Minor)  Ewha Womans’ University, Seoul, South Korea |
| 2002.1\~2002.6 | Language Institute of Indiana university at South Bend, United States |
| 2002.6\~2002.8 | Indiana University at South Bend, one summer session |
| 2003.9\~2004.5 | Exchange Student for one year at Thiel College Greenville, Indiana  This record is written in the transcript of academic record for a bachelor’s degree.  |
| 2003.12\~2004.1 | Queretaro language Institute, Exchange Student , Querétaro, Mexico |
| 2004.7\~2005.6 | Beijing Language and Culture University |
| 2014.3-2016.2 | Awarded Master Degree in Department of Tourism and Convention, The graduate school, Pusan National University, Pusan, South Korea “Analyzing tourism movement patterns using data mining: Comparative analysis of movement characteristics by tourism motivation” |
| 2016.3-2018.8 | Completion of a doctoral course, Pusan National University, Pusan  |
| 2020.1-2022.5 | University of the Sunshine Coast, Queensland, AustraliaPh.D. in Tourism, 2022 *Dissertation:* Development of a scale to measure destination social responsibility and the co-orientation analysis in the perception of destination social responsibility DOI: [https://doi.org/10.25907/00144](https://doi.org/10.25907/00144) |

**A-2. Employment and Professional Experience**

* **Consular Support Intern, Korean Consulate in Beijing** – Beijing, China  
  *Jul. 2005 – Aug. 2005*  
  * Assisted with consular affairs and supported administrative operations and helped facilitate communication with Korean nationals in China


  * **Hotel Sales & Marketing associate, Jungsang Bukhan Mountain Resort** (Hotel Paraspaspa) – Seoul, South Korea  
    *2022 – Present (non-full-time appointment)*  
* Developed and implemented B2B/B2C sales strategies for a luxury resort property  
* Led targeted marketing campaigns   
* Collaborated with tourism platforms, tour operators, and hospitality businesses to expand market reach  
* Supported the resort’s ESG initiatives by integrating sustainability-focused messaging into marketing content and promoting eco-friendly tourism products and experiences

**A-3. Teaching Experience**

* Mar. 2024 – Present: Adjunct Professor, Department of Future Convergence, Silla University — Busan, South Korea

Teaching undergraduate courses in tourism and global cultural content  
Courses taught:

* Spring Semester: *Wellness Tourism*, *Global Travel and Etiquette*  
* Fall Semester: *Green Tourism*, *World City Travel: Exploring Festivals and Local Cuisine*

**A-4. Extra activities and License** 

* Teacher’s license Ewha University No. 20233 (for teaching English at Middle school and high school)  
* Social Service Certificate 2006-1-0583: Volunteer activities for civic groups ⅠⅡⅢ (from 2001 fall semester to 2002 spring semester & autumn semester)  
* Taekwondo Certified 1st rank  
* Ewha Samsung Essence Skill Development Training Course (2003.7.1-2003.7.3)  
* HSK nine rank (2005.12)  
* Korean History Test 1st rank (2011.5.17)


**A-5. Main Achievements and award**

* Published more than 15 refereed articles in journals and 12 conference proceedings  
* (including one in A in ABDC).   
* Received \[Global PhD Fellowship\] Full PhD scholarship from 2016.3/2019.2.  
* Global Ph.D. Fellowship is equivalent to the Endeavor Scholarship and Fellowship.   
* 2015\. 05\. 27 Awarded ‘Academic Award of the Year in Pusan National University’ in 2015– Master’s student in Economics and Trade graduate school  
* ​2018\. 05\. 23 ‘Academic Award of the Year in Pusan National University’ in 2018 – Doctoral student Master’s student in Economics and Trade graduate school  
* The Best Paper Awards the 1st Global Congress of Special Interest Tourism and Hospitality (GLOSITH)

**A-6. Main Research Interests**

* Tourism policy and strategic planning  
* Sustainable development and tourism planning  
* Hotel and accommodation management  
* Wellness and health tourism  
* MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conventions, and Exhibitions) industry  
* Urban leisure content  
* Marine tourism and coastal destination studies  
* Cultural issues in tourism and hospitality  
* Innovation, digital marketing, and tourism technologies  
* Customer service and experience management  
* Social responsibility in the tourism and hospitality industry  
* Tourist behavior and spatial mobility

| Research Area | Supporting Background |
| :---- | :---- |
| Tourism policy and strategic planning | Directly related to Ph.D. dissertation |
| Sustainable development and tourism planning | Core component of Ph.D. research |

| Urban leisure content | Matches the academic focus of target institutions; further output in progress |

| Innovation, digital marketing, and tourism technologies | Connected to published research on destination network analysis; more contributions planned |

5. **Supplementary Teaching Roles**

- I have an experience to teach English classes at a high school in Korea with the official license of teaching English (Teacher’s license Ewha University No. 20233).  
- I taught English conversation for ten hours at the Samjung high school, Pusan, South Korea in January 7-22 in 2019\.   
- I have taught English at the Samjung high school, South Korea as a part of undergraduate program of “student teaching” during the spring semester in 2006\.   
  This experience could be proved in the academic record for a bachelor degree in the name of  “**student teaching**” class during the first semester in 2006
